title: Fetterman threatens to abandon Democrats
description: Sen. John Fetterman (D-Pa.) said he'd leave the Democratic Party if it becomes the "anti-Israel party," though he signaled he had no plans to imminently depart the caucus.Why it matters: Republicans currently hold a narrow Senate majority, but with midterm elections looming, Fetterman's comments could be a blow to Democrats should they flip four seats in November and regain control. Fetterman acknowledged the Democrats' difficult Senate math when

title: Oil falls to 3-1/2-month low after US, Iran sign ceasefire deal
description:
BEIJING/SINGAPORE: Oil prices ​fell by more than $2 per barrel on Thursday after the US and Iran signed an interim agreement that ‌would end the Iran war, reopen the Strait of Hormuz and waive US sanctions on Tehran’s oil, boosting the oil supply outlook.

	Brent crude futures were down $2.14, or 2.69 percent, at $77.41 a barrel as of 09:16 a.m. Saudi time, and US West Texas Intermediate fell $2.36, or 3.07 percent, to $74.43 a barrel.

title: Trump, Netanyahu clash over Iran strategy and Lebanon ceasefire
description: DUBAI: American President Donald Trump and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu have found themselves at odds over Iran, Lebanon and the course of ongoing negotiations in the Middle East according to The Washington Post.

title: US will be candid in push for ‘hard-line’ NATO alliance, Hegseth says
description:
BRUSSELS: NATO needs to return to its roots as a “hard-line” military alliance and the United States will be candid in public and ‌private about ‌countries that ‌need to ⁠do more to ⁠meet their commitments, US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said on Thursday at ⁠NATO headquarters.
	“(There are) ‌some ‌that still need ‌to do more, ‌and we will be candid about that, both in private ‌and in public. I think ⁠that’s important, ⁠friends being honest wit

title: US approves nearly $2bn in weapons sale to Saudi Arabia
description: The sale intends to bolster Saudi air defence systems as the kingdom risks escalation with the Iran-backed Houthis.

description: Rare protests erupted in central Kyiv over Ukrainian President Zelenskyy’s dismissal of defence minister.

title: Americans keep spending while "grumbling" about the economy

Yet their willingness to spend has not yet cracked.Why it matters: Economists feared that the Middle East-driven jump in gasoline prices would dent consumer spending. Instead, a still-stable labor market and rising household wealth appear to have given consumers enough cushion to keep spending.For Federal Reserve officials,

title: Langley F-22s Return Home from Operation Epic Fury Deployment
description: Deployed to Israel since February, F-22 Raptors from the 1st Fighter Wing at Langley AFB transited towards home with an unusual stopover at RAF Fairford over the weekend. Twelve 1st FW Raptors in total are thought to be part of the package heading home. Ten jets made it to RAF Fairford on Jul. 10, 2026, []

title: Lawmakers demand Pentagon release findings from probe of Iran school strike
description: Lawmakers demanded Monday that the Pentagon release its findings from an investigation into the Feb. 28 strike at a girls’ school in Iran.

title: 'Zero tolerance' for forest arsonists, Macron promises firefighters
description: French President Emmenuel Macron visited Fontainebleau on the outskirts of Paris, after devastating wildfires that scorched at least 10% of the 20-thousand-hectare forest. Macron warned it would ‌take several weeks for the fire to be completely ⁠extinguished but pledged to replant the historic domain. Meanwhile, several arrests have been made, including one of a volunteer firefighter placed under formal investigation.

title: Mykhailo Fedorov: Ousted defence chief's reforms riled Ukrainian army bosses
description: Ukraine's ousted defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov served just six months before his ambitious drive to reform the military led to irreconcilable clashes with the country's top brass, which he viewed as out of date, inflexible and mired in bureaucracy. Fedorov's early bet on drone warfare was a game-changer as Ukraine faced off against a more formidable opponent.

title: Israeli attacks kill at least five Palestinians in Gaza despite ‘ceasefire’
description: Attacks continue in violation of the October agreement with more than 1,100 Palestinians killed since then.

title: Hamas leadership run-off expected between Meshaal and al-Hayya
description: Israeli assassinations of several key leaders force Hamas to find new head, a process expected to be completed next week

title: Zelensky's removal of popular defence minister sparks protests in Ukraine
description: A rift between Fedorov and Commander-in-Chief Oleksandr Syrskyi was apparently at the heart of Zelensky's decision.

title: Израильские военные заявили, что контролируют более 60% сектора Газа
description: Армия обороны Израиля (ЦАХАЛ) на данный момент держит под контролем свыше 60% сектора Газа, заявил командующий Центральным военным округом генерал-майор Ави Блут, его слова приводит The Times of Israel."Это зона безопасности возле границ с сектором Газа, которую удерживают две дивизии и лучшие солдаты ЦАХАЛ.

title: Суд приговорил подростков к 5,5 года тюрьмы за взлом системы транспорта Лондона
description: Королевский суд Вулиджа вынес приговор по делу о кибератаке на госкомпанию Transport of London, управляющую большей частью общественного транспорта и дорожной сети британской столицы. Два хакера, которым во время совершения преступления было 17 и 18 лет, приговорены к пяти с половиной годам тюрьмы.

title: Franco-German defense cooperation under strain as Macron, Merz meet
description: The meetings come weeks after industrial rivalries forced Paris and Berlin to scrap the landmark Future Combat Air System (FCAS) fighter jet program.

title: Middle East war rekindles as US and Iran resume strike exchange
description: The United States and Iran continued to trade blows for a sixth day on July 16, as the foes battled over the vital Strait of Hormuz, despite a call from mediator Pakistan to resume talks. The renewed fighting and US blockade of Iranian ports came a month after a preliminary deal was signed that aimed to end the conflict. Since last week, the renewed US attacks have killed at least 30 people in Iran, according to its government.

title: Washington is ready to 'upend the rules-based world order', HRW chief says
description: In an interview with FRANCE 24, Human Rights Watch executive director Philippe Bolopion says the Trump administration has "launched an all-out assault on human rights" at home and abroad. He calls the Fort Bliss ICE detention centre a "human rights catastrophe" and warns Washington is ready to "upend the rules-based world order".

title: An individually curated tour of the City of Lights
description: Paris is one of the most visited cities in the world with some 50 million tourists exploring the French capital every year. Many visitors are coming back for a second or third time, and – having already ticked off the must-see sites – are looking for new ways to see the city. In this edition, Maud Hacker, art historian and private tour guide, tells us a little about her bespoke tours and how any traveller to Paris can craft their own unique exper

title: Both UK and Ukraine say goodbye to Keir Starmer
description: Ukraine awarded Britain's Keir Starmer its "Order of Freedom" honour on July 19 as the outgoing prime minister made his final visit to the capital, Kyiv. Burnham will become leader of the ruling Labour party on July 17 before taking over as prime minister on July 20. He will be the UK's fifth leader since Russia invaded its neighbour in February 2022. FRANCE 24's Clovis Casali looks back at Starmer's policy regarding Ukraine.

title: Lebanon and Israel conclude round of talks on IDF withdrawal
description: Syria said on July 19 it had foiled an attempt to smuggle weapons to Hezbollah in Lebanon by seizing a shipment that included missiles at its border with Iraq. It comes after US President Donald Trump floated the idea of Syria intervening in Lebanon against the armed groupn, and amid talks between Beirut and Tel Aviv in Rome over the withdrawal of Israeli forces from the south of Lebanon. FRANCE 24's Renée Davis tells us more.

title: As the war rekindles, what options are left to the US and Iran?
description: The US and Iran have rekindled their tit-for-tat strikes as both battle over control of the Strait of Hormuz. It comes a month after a preliminary deal was signed to end the conflict. Kuwait and Bahrain faced renewed aerial attacks from Iran, with Theran saying it was targeting US military bases in the Gulf. But how long can that conflict last? FRANCE 24's Angela Diffley tells us more.

title: Ukraine: Protests erupt after defence minister sacked
description: With demonstrators shouting “Shame!” and “Power to the people!”, rare protests erupted in several Ukrainian cities on Thursday over the dismissal of the country’s popular defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov. Seen as a moderniser, Fedorov was removed by President Volodymyr Zelensky after only six months in the job in what many see as a result of infighting within the Ukrainian military.

title: Ousted defence chief Mykhailo Fedorov, whose reforms riled Ukraine's army bosses
description: Ukraine's ousted defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov served just six months before his ambitious drive to reform the military led to irreconcilable clashes with the country's top brass. He increasingly battled an army command he cast as out of date, inflexible and mired in bureaucracy.

title: Macron vows to replant Fontainebleau forest after devastating wildfires
description: French President Emmanuel Macron on Thursday vowed to replant Fontainebleau forest after devastating wildfires blazed through at least 10% of the 20,000 hectare demain and forced hundreds of residents to flee. The historic forest, just south of Paris, draws hikers, climbers and nature lovers from all over the world every year.

title: France explored Pegasus spyware deal despite Morocco espionage claims
description: France briefly explored acquiring the controversial Pegasus spyware in 2019, according to media investigations, even as Morocco was allegedly using the Israeli-made surveillance tool to target French ministers. The report claims President Emmanuel Macron ultimately rejected the purchase to avoid reliance on foreign technology. Morocco has denied the spying allegations.

title: 'Thousands of people are still dying over a few meters of ground' in Ukraine

He has spoken with FRANCE 24 multiple times to share some of the still photographs he has taken in Ukraine – both in major cities like Kyiv and from the frontline. title: How a fuming Trump reversed ICE's pause on vehicle stops
description: Late Tuesday at the White House, a livid President Trump complained to advisers about the Department of Homeland Security's decision to pause the type of vehicle stops by ICE agents that had led to two fatal shootings in the past two weeks.The department's idea seemed to be that agents would get more training. But by Wednesday morning, Trump had sent a Truth Social post reversing the pause, leaving DHS Secretary Markwayne Mullin and border czar T

title: Vance enters the media lion's den
description: Vice President Vance is venturing into unfriendly media terrain to defend the White House against its critics — reviving the same playbook that endeared him to President Trump when he was picking his running mate.Why it matters: Vance is using the media blitz to showcase one of his biggest political assets — his talent for verbal combat — as he lays the groundwork for an expected 2028 presidential campaign.His approach contrasts with other high-p

title: Ahead of French PM's visit, Rabat faces allegations of spying on Paris
description: French Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu is set to visit Morocco on July 16 in an effort to strengthen ties between Paris and Rabat, whether it is for trade or defence. But as the visit starts, an investigation has revealed that Morocco has used Israeli spyware to spy on several French ministers, including the French prime minister himself. FRANCE 24's Shirli Sitbon tells us more.

title: A week of strikes shatters hope of a lasting US-Iran peace deal
description: Hopes of a lasting peace deal between Iran and the US are fading fast following a week that has seen consistent exchange of fire between the two sides. The US resumed its blockade on the Strait of Hormuz firing at a ship that attempted to breach it. For its part, Iran retaliated by launching strikes on US allies including Jordan, where projectiles believed to be of Iranian origin were spotted.

title: Senegal's 'plastic man' helps raise awareness about plastic pollution
description: In West Africa, plastic waste is a major environmental challenge. In Senegal, activist Modou Fall has taken an unusual approach to raising awareness about plastic pollution. He walks through Dakar and other parts of the country wearing an eye-catching outfit made from discarded plastic waste. His goal is to highlight the scale of the problem and urge authorities to enforce environmental protection laws.

title: Occupied West Bank school protests planned demolition by Israeli forces
description: Students and teachers at a primary school in the Israeli-occupied West Bank demonstrated on July 15 against its expected demolition by Israeli forces. Israel has in recent years destroyed thousands of Palestinian-owned homes & vital infrastructures, usually citing a lack of building permits. Actions which have repeatedly been condemned by human rights organisations and the international community.

World Cup 2026: Huge crowds in Argentina celebrate victory over England
description: Hundreds of thousands of Argentina fans packed the streets of Buenos Aires on Wednesday night to celebrate victory over England and a place in the World Cup final. Defending champions Argentina scored two late goals to come from behind and win 2-1. They will now meet Spain in Sunday’s final.

title: French police investigate suspected pyromaniacs thought to have started Fontainebleau fire
description: With the two wildfires burning southeast of Paris now contained, for investigators, the focus is on how they started. Five people have been arrested, suspected of setting the blazes intentionally, including a volunteer fire fighter. Prosecutors say that a second suspect admitted to accidentally starting a fire by throwing away his cigarette.

title: Ukrainians protest the dismissal of Kyiv's 'most successful defence minister'
description: Russian missiles targeted the Ukrainian capital early on July 16, but it hasn't deterred protesters who are out in the streets opposing the dismissal of the popular defence minister, Mykhailo Fedorov. title: Venezuelans dig to find their loved ones
description: Two earthquakes of rare magnitude struck Venezuela on June 24, seconds apart. The state of La Guaira on the Caribbean coast was by far the hardest hit, with entire buildings collapsing there in seconds. Tens of thousands of people are still missing but help still has not arrived.

title: Protests erupt in Ukraine as Zelensky removes defence minister, 'his most popular'
description: Large protests erupted in several Ukrainian cities on July 16 against the removal of popular Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov by President Volodymyr Zelensky. Only appointed six months ago, Fedorov carved a reputation as a moderniser who tried to reform the Ukrainian military, worn down by more than four years of the Russian invasion. FRANCE 24's Emmanuelle Chaze tells us more from one of the protests.

title: Protests against removal of popular defence minister erupt across Ukraine
description: Rare wartime protests in Ukraine erupted across the country on Thursday with demonstrators demanding that President Volodymyr Zelensky reappoint the popular Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov, who was removed from office on Wednesday. Lawmakers are set to vote on a new government today.

description: Facing intense divisions within Iran, Gen. Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf both struck a defiant tone and argued that diplomacy could defend Iran’s interests.

title: Measure to cut Israel aid fails in bipartisan House vote
description: A measure to prevent any State Department funding from going to Israel failed by a decisive margin in a House vote on Wednesday.Why it matters: The vote exposed deep divisions among House Democrats, with even the party's top two leaders taking divergent positions.House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) and 97 other Democrats voted against the amendment, along with 215 Republicans.But Minority Whip Katherine Clark (D-Mass.) — along with 102

title: Fedorov out as Ukraine’s defense minister in major government shake-up
description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy dismissed Mykhailo Fedorov as the country's minister of defense after just six months in office.

title: Europe’s defense build-up is delivering for NATO — and America
description: "We invite our American friends to judge us not by old assumptions, but by the record we are building together," writes the EU top envoy to the U.S.

title: Russian attacks kill 14 as Ukraine hits Black Sea oil tankers
description: Civilian casualties are reported in a number of Ukrainian regions, while Kyiv hits 20 Russian vessels in the Black Sea.

title: What are the obstacles to peace between Israel and Lebanon?
description: Another round of talks is under way between Israel and Lebanon to try to advance the ceasefire agreement.

description: President Masoud Pezeshkian vowed Iran will defend every inch of its territory, saying attempts to tear the country apar

title: Ukraine’s Minister of Defense, a Proponent of Drone War, Steps Down
description: Mykhailo Fedorov announced he was leaving the ministry on Wednesday after conflicts with generals and military contractors over the role of innovative robot and drone weapons in war.

title: Helsing raises $1.8 billion in Europe’s biggest defense-startup round
description: Defense technology has been one of the fastest-growing areas of venture capital funding on the back of rising military budgets, and in Europe especially.

title: Europe’s Hydis project settles on concept for hypersonic interceptor
description: Europe's push to field a hypersonic-defense interceptor will come down to two competing projects.

title: People globally view China's Xi more favourably than Donald Trump, Pew poll finds
description: More people have favourable views of China than the United States in 25 out of 36 countries and territories surveyed, including Canada and Mexico, according to a new poll by the Pew Research Center released on Wednesday. Views were also more favourable of Chinese leader Xi Jinping than US President Donald Trump in 22 of the countries and territories.

title: Russia pounds Ukraine with missile, drone strikes
description: Footage shows firefighters extinguishing a massive fire after Russia launched air strikes on Ukraine.

title: Ukraine strikes 20 Russian vessels
description: Ukraine released video of strikes on 20 Russian vessels in the Black Sea, including 17 oil tankers.

title: The history of the US and Iraq’s complicated relationship
description: As Iraq’s prime minister visits Washington seeking deeper economic ties.

title: Protests in Paris, Gaza for release of Hussam Abu Safia
description: Protests were held in Paris and Gaza demanding the release of Palestinian doctor Hussam Abu Safia.

title: Billboard in Iran’s Capital Depicts Trump in a Coffin
description: Threatening imagery in Enghelab Square is nothing new, but has rarely taken such lurid form.

title: Vance: "We did" mishandle Epstein files release
description: Vice President JD Vance said the Trump administration "mishandled" last year's release of the Epstein files during an appearance on "The Joe Rogan Experience."Why it matters: Vance's comments are among the clearest public acknowledgments yet from the Trump administration that the Epstein files release became a major political liability.Many noted at the time that the Epstein files release was done alongside clumsy messaging and puzzling redaction

title: Ukraine’s newest strike weapon drifts into Russia on the wind
description: The U.S. and Russia are fielding the same balloons used by Ukraine that bait air defenses and now launch missiles.

title: Senate eyes Hegseth travel cuts without probes into Iran school bombing, boat strikes
description: Senate lawmakers added a provision in the NDAA that would restrict Hegseth's travel expenses until the department releases certain operational information.

title: Ukraine hits Moscow refinery in major drone attack on Russian capital
description: Ukrainian drones hit the Russian capital’s oil refinery for the second time this week.

title: US approves Kuwait request to buy nearly $2 billion of counter-drone platforms
description: Following recent Iranian strikes on Kuwait, the U.S. approved a potential foreign military sale of counter-UAS platforms made by Anduril.

title: Firefighters battle 'contained' Fontainebleau forest fire near Paris amid arson probe
description: With the two wildfires burning southeast of Paris now contained, for investigators, the focus is on how they started. Five people have been arrested, suspected of setting the blazes intentionally, including a volunteer fire fighter. Prosecutors say that a second suspect admitted to accidentally starting a fire by throwing away his cigarette. FRANCE 24's Monte Francis reports.

title: US launches ‘wave of strikes’ on Iran, Tehran threatens to halt regional energy exports
description: The US military on Wednesday launched rare daytime attacks on Iran, including a 90-minute wave of strikes, as it reimposed a naval blockade on Iranian ports. Tehran responded with strikes on “US military targets” in Gulf states and threatened to halt energy exports in the region.

title: EU, Ukraine seal 'drone deal' to boost production, establish joint ventures
description: EU chief Ursula von der Leyen on Wednesday announced a "drone deal" with Ukraine to boost joint production of the key battlefield technology. Ukraine has already signed similar deals with some individual EU countries and Gulf nations looking to tap into the expertise Kyiv has developed during more than four years of war with Russia. FRANCE 24's EU correspondent Dave Keating has more.

title: Fourteen-year-old boy charged over alleged plot targeting London mosques
description: Suspect is charged with preparation of terrorist acts - linked to extreme right-wing terrorism, say British police.

title: Argentina fans flood Atlanta ahead of England World Cup match
description: Thousands of Argentina fans held a banderazo in the streets of Atlanta ahead of their World Cup semi-final.

title: European Athletics moves to curb on-screen sexualisation of female athletes
description: The guidelines are based on feedback from athletes who said certain camera shots can cause discomfort and distractions.

title: Palestinian homes sealed off by Israeli military restrictions
description: An Israeli military order imposed after a series of settler attacks has left families cut off from parts of their home.

title: Lebanon and Israel end sixth round of US-brokered talks
description: A US official said the two sides made progress on a plan for Israel to withdraw from pilot zones in southern Lebanon.

title: Iran unveils huge billboard showing Trump in coffin
description: Iran has installed a billboard in the capital Tehran showing US President Donald Trump lying in a coffin.

title: U.S. Strikes Hit Greater Tunb Island in Strait of Hormuz
description: The U.S. military said on Wednesday it had attacked Iranian cruise missile storage and launch sites on Greater Tunb, one of several small islands in the Strait of Hormuz subject to a decades-old territorial dispute.

title: U.S. Employs Armed Surface Drones In Combat For The First Time During Strikes On Iranian Naval Base
description: The attack on Bandar Abbas marked the first confirmed combat use of U.S. unmanned surface vessels (USVs), with three Saronic Corsair drone boats striking a submarine and ship maintenance facility. The U.S. military has confirmed that it employed armed Unmanned Surface Vessels (USVs) in combat for the first time on July 12, 2026. According to []

title: Ukraine agrees on plan to acquire 16 Rafale jets, France’s Macron says
description: Ukraine has agreed on a plan to acquire 16 Rafale fighter jets from France together with the accompanying weapon systems, along with air-defense equipment.

title: Following years of wildfires, Europe must 'diversify' landscapes and strengthen land management
description: For Spotlight, François Picard is pleased to welcome Johan Sjöström, Senior Research Scientist at RISE Research Institutes of Sweden. He argues that the devastating wildfires affecting northern France are not an isolated anomaly but part of a "new normal" for Europe. His focus moves beyond extreme weather to examine the interaction between climate, vegetation, land management and institutional preparedness.

title: Fast food nation? French chicken chain row gets political
description: On this particular television channel we do love covering the French and their love affair with fine food. But truth be told, eating habits here vary wildly be it by necessity, choice or habit. Don't say it too loudly but France boasts Europe’s highest number of McDonalds per person. So here we go with the feud between neighboring Paris suburbs over chicken drumsticks.

title: More people around the world now favour China over the US, Pew study suggests
description: There is more confidence in Xi Jinping than Donald Trump, the US think tank's survey indicates.

title: Mapping the latest US strikes across Iran
description: US attacks kill dozens in Iran as Iranian forces target regional bases, pushing a fragile ceasefire closer to collapse.

title: House Democratic leaders face rare divide over vote to cut off U.S. aid to Israel
description: House Democrats' internal split over a vote to cut off U.S. aid to Israel has extended to leadership, with Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) and Minority Whip Katherine Clark (D-Mass.) planning to vote differently.Why it matters: Such a divergence between the caucus' top two leaders is exceedingly rare and underscores just how much anxiety this vote is giving some in the party.Many House Democrats have substantive misgivings but plan to vo

title: Warsh: AI spending may lift prices without fueling lasting inflation
description: Federal Reserve chairman Kevin Warsh said on Wednesday that the AI investment boom will likely raise prices over the next year, but argued that those increases might not automatically be inflationary. Why it matters: Warsh drew one of his clearest distinctions yet between the AI boom's immediate price effects and persistent inflation — a nuance that could shape policymakers' response as investment remains strong.What they're saying: "Will it incr

title: MBDA appoints Airbus executive Jean-Brice Dumont as new CEO
description: MBDA, Europe’s largest missile maker, appointed Airbus Defence and Space executive Jean-Brice Dumont as its new chief executive officer effective Nov. 1.

title: 🇦🇲🇷🇺Armenia - Russia: Armenian apricots caught in a geopolitical battle
description: In Armenia’s Ararat valley, apricots are getting swept into the centre of a geopolitical battle between Russia and the West. Angered at Yerevan's pivot away from Moscow, the Kremlin imposed bans and restrictions on the imports of a wide range of Armenian goods, including fruits, vegetables and flowers, ahead of parliamentary elections last month.

title: US-Iran: Why the cycle of war, talks, repeat?
description:
🇮🇷🇺🇸 Why keep reigniting the conflict?
 
German Marshall Fund of the US' Carrie Lee argues the #IranWar persists because both sides seemingly believe continuous conflict gains them greater leverage in negotiations. More on #F24Debate➡️https://f24.my/C3TH

title: Europe: National elections may bring "very different Coalition of the Willing next year"
description:
🇺🇦 "There could be very different representation at the coalition of the willing next year."
 
Kira Rudik assesses how political support for #Ukraine may shift with the results of key upcoming #European national elections. More on #F24Debate➡️https://f24.my/C3LG

title: Does Marco Rubio control Venezuela?
description: Does Marco Rubio covertly control Venezuela? A report from the New York Times suggests that the US Secretary of State now effectively controls the Latin American country's finances, the distribution of its natural resources and its government since the capture of Nicolas Maduro. FRANCE 24's Monte Francis speaks with Christopher Sabatini, Director of Chatham House’s Latin America program.

title: Why the US is launching naval drones at Iran’s port city of Bandar Abbas
description: The US has pounded Iran’s southern port city of Bandar Abbas since Tehran and Washington renewed missile strikes last week, including an unprecedented sea drone attack on a submarine maintenance facility. We take a look at why this city of more than half a million people has become a prime target for the US military.

title: Pro-government militia seizes boat with ‘Houthi weapons materials’ in Yemen
description: A pro-government militia said it intercepted a boat carrying ‘weapon-making equipment for the Houthis’ in Yemen.

title: Revamped Cricket World Cup format could see an additional India-Pakistan match
description: The 14-team 2027 ODI tournament in Africa will have a Super Seven stage but no quarterfinals.

title: Atlanta police gear up for trouble at England vs Argentina World Cup match
description: The host city will have advanced security in place as a proactive measure against any potential crowd trouble between fans.

title: Cruise ship hotel left listing in China port after high tides
description: A cruise ship-turned-luxury floating hotel that has been idle for about 14 years rolled onto its side while docked.

title: Zelenskyy backs state energy company chief Koretskyi for new Ukraine PM
description: Ukraines parliament is expected to vote for the next prime minister on Thursday.

title: Father, mother, daughter killed in Israeli attack on Gaza home
description: Palestinians held a funeral for a father, a mother and their six-year-old daughter killed in an Israeli air attack.

title: US launches new round of strikes against Iran
description: The U.S. conducted a new wave of strikes against Iranian military sites on Wednesday after reimposing a naval blockade of the Islamic Republic's ports.

title: First the coast guard, then an ICBM: China tests new, long-term ways to hold off rivals in Asia
description: Experts say Beijing used both methods to try out new, attention-grabbing and long-term ways to deter others from challenging its territorial claims.

title: Gibraltar and Spain end border checks in boost for businesses
description: Thousands of people who travel every day between the southern tip of Spain and the British territory of Gibraltar will no longer have to cross a physical border, beginning on Wednesday. The official opening at midnight on Tuesday, after a border fence was removed, allows a new freedom of movement under a historic treaty between the European Union and the United Kingdom and will come as a welcome boost for local businesses.

title: Briton jailed in Iran given additional two-year sentence, family says
description: The family ⁠of British ​couple Craig and Lindsay Foreman, who are serving a 10-year ​prison sentence in Iran on espionage charges that they deny, said on Wednesday that a judge had extended Craig Foreman's ​sentence ‌by two years for speaking to the ⁠media. The couple were arrested in 2025 while travelling through Iran by motorcycle ‌as part of a round-the-world journey. Britain has described ⁠their original sentences as "totally unjustifiable".

title: Who is calling the shots in Iran amid the deadly war with the US?
description: The IRGC has taken the lead in Iran, particularly over Hormuz - and the government says its on the same page.

title: How US-Iran escalation will test Iraq’s balancing act
description: The Iraqi PM visited the White House to deepen economic ties, as Iraq-based allies of Iran threatened to fight the US.

title: Wildfires Rage in Ontario as Smoke Affects Toronto’s Air Quality
description: Air quality in Canada’s largest city was at times the worst in the world on Wednesday, as wildfires across Ontario sent smoke drifting into New York City and beyond.

title: Special police team to investigate killing of an anti-migrant leader in South Africa
description: The March and March provincial leader died of his injuries in hospital several days after being shot.

title: U.S. Presses China to Free American Seismologist Accused of Spying
description: President Trump has urged Xi Jinping to release the scientist, Youlin Chen, the man’s family says. Dr. Chen studied the detection of North Korean nuclear tests.

title: Ukraine Pounds Russian Ships in Its Campaign to Cut Off Crimea
description: After striking roads and railways, Kyiv is now focusing on sea routes as it tries to disrupt fuel supplies and pressure Russia to end the war.

title: For Israel, the U.S.-Iran Hostilities Have Created an Uneasy Limbo
description: Officials see a return to full-blown war as preferable to an agreement that fails to curb the threats Iran poses to Israel. Meanwhile, they wait.

title: Iran War Live Updates: U.S. and Iran Exchange Strikes for Fifth Straight Day
description: The United States launched fresh attacks against Iranian targets on Wednesday, hours after Iran targeted American military sites in the region. Neither side has shown signs of backing down as they enter a new stage of the war.

title: Kyiv Independent: США начали процесс выдачи Киеву лицензии на ракеты Patriot
description: Соединённые Штаты уже начали процесс выдачи Киеву лицензии на производство ракет для систем Patriot. Об этом сообщил газете The Kyiv Independent высокопоставленный украинский чиновник. Читать далее

title: Exclusive: Bank of America CEO bullish on economy powered by spending
description: Affordability pain is real, but resilient consumers are still infusing money into the economy, Brian Moynihan, CEO of Bank of America, said at the Axios House News Shapers summit in Washington, D.C.Why it matters: The U.S. economy sits on a remarkably durable foundation, supporting consumer spending even with hot inflation. But at the same time, Americans tell pollsters economic conditions is at rock bottom, fueling a political reckoning over aff

title: T. rex skeleton fetches record $50.1 mn at US auction
description: A Tyrannosaurus rex skeleton nicknamed "Gus" sold Tuesday for $50.1 million at Sotheby's in New York, making it the most valuable dinosaur fossil bought at auction after a 10-minute battle between seven bidders. "Gus" is one of the world's most complete T. rex skeletons -- with 183 fossilized bones -- and was discovered on a cattle ranch in South Dakota in 2021.

title: Shock and solidarity after wildfires ravage Fontainebleau
description: France 24's Claire Paccalin reports from Fontainebleau near Paris, where wildfires that have devastated large parts of the region's forest have been contained, but not yet extinguished. She says locals there have been left shocked by the blazes in a part of France not usually hit by forest fires.

title: Trump threatens strikes on Iran's bridges and power plants
description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday reimposed a naval blockade of all Iranian ports and threatened to hit power plants and bridges next week unless Tehran resumes negotiations, in the latest U.S. escalation of the conflict.

title: Expert warns 'European temperatures are warming the fastest'
description: In this edition, a climatologist talks to FRANCE 24 about our changing weather patterns and their effects on wildfires currently raging across Europe. Dr. Alan Kennedy-Asser, Senior researcher at the University of Bristol, sheds light on the issue against a backdrop of hundreds of French firefighters just managing to contain two fires that have raged across parts of the famous Fontainebleau forest near Paris.

title: Far-right Israeli lawmaker raids Palestinian school and threatens shutdown
description: Israel’s education committee head stormed a school in Jerusalem, vowing to shut it down over Palestinian symbols.

title: Protest in Pakistan’s Kashmir stalls after nine killed in clashes
description: At least 28 people have been killed since protests began in June, as the region braces for a major protest march.

title: US strikes marine control tower in Iran
description: US strikes marine control tower in Iran

title: As the US restarts war on Iran, is its weapons stockpile running low?
description: The US has expended a significant number of munitions since the war began in February.

title: Will Lindsey Graham’s death affect Israel’s standing in Washington?
description: It might in the short term, but not in the long term.

title: Israeli air attack on Gaza apartment kills family of three
description: One child survives hit on Deir el-Balah home as Israel escalates attacks across territory despite October ceasefire.

title: India-UK trade deal comes into effect: What’s cheaper in each country now?
description: The deal cuts tariffs on thousands of goods and widens access for services firms and ​professionals in both markets.

title: Iraq’s prime minister carries the title, but not the power
description: Al-Zaidi’s Washington visit exposed the pressure reshaping Baghdad’s politics, security and economy.

title: Russian attacks kill eight as Ukraine hits Black Sea oil tankers
description: Civilian casualties are reported in four Ukrainian regions, while Kyiv hits 20 Russian vessels in the Black Sea.

title: Brazen Japanese Bears Are Breaking Into Homes and Raiding Pantries
description: Climate change and shifting demographics are driving the country’s Asiatic black bears closer to humans.

title: Агентам ICE запретили задерживать мигрантов в автомобилях
description: Сотрудникам Службы иммиграции и таможенного контроля США (ICE) временно запретили задерживать мигрантов при проверке автомобилей. Как сообщает The Washington Post (WP) со ссылкой на источники, запрет введен после нескольких случаев, когда сотрудники ICE в ходе досмотров открывали огонь и убивали находившихся там людей.

title: Крупнейшие авиакомпании Китая потеряли $1,3 млрд из-за подорожавшего авиатоплива
description: Три крупнейшие государственные авиакомпании Китая China Southern Airlines, Air China и China Eastern Airlines уведомили регуляторов об ожидаемых убытках по итогам первого полугодия в размере от 7,37 млрд до 8,97 млрд юаней (от $1,1 млрд до $1,33 млрд). Об этом сообщает агентство Nikkei.

title: Israel says won’t withdraw troops from Lebanon, Syria, Gaza
description:
JERUSALEM: Israel’s defense minister said Monday that Israeli forces would remain in Lebanon, Syria and Gaza indefinitely, hours after the United States and Iran agreed to end the Middle East war, including in Lebanon.
	“Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and I are pursuing a clear policy under which the IDF will remain in the security zones in Lebanon, Syria and Gaza for an unlimited period of time, in order to protect the border and Israeli comm

title: Lebanon warns displaced against rushing home after US-Iran deal
description:
BEIRUT/JERUSALEM: Authorities in southern Lebanon warned people displaced by three months of war between Israel and Hezbollah against rushing home on Monday despite a US-Iran deal to end the wider conflict, as Israel said it would not withdraw troops from the south.

	The warning comes as Lebanon has not been informed of details of the US-Iran agreement, an official source told AFP on Monday.

	“Lebanon was not informed of the terms of the agreem

title: Gulf, Arab states welcome US-Iran deal to end war, reopen Hormuz
description:
DUBAI: Gulf and Arab states welcomed on Monday a US-Iran agreement to end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, expressing hope that the deal will pave the way for lasting regional stability and broader diplomatic engagement.

	Saudi Arabia welcomed the agreement between Washington and Tehran to halt military operations and begin detailed negotiations over a 60-day period aimed at reaching a permanent settlement.

title: Oil hits 3-month low as US, Iran reach peace deal to reopen Strait of Hormuz
description:
SINGAPORE: Oil prices ​slipped to a three-month low on Monday after US President Donald Trump and Iran's deputy foreign minister said ‌they had reached an initial deal to end the war and to resume traffic through the Strait of Hormuz.

	Brent crude futures fell $3.65, or 4.2 percent, to $83.68 a barrel by 09:30 a.m. and US West Texas Intermediate was at $80.75, down $4.13, or 4.9 percent. Both contracts fell to their lowest ​levels since March 10

title: Ukraine to Receive 16 Rafales and License for SCALP and Aster-30 Production
description: Ukraine will procure 16 Rafales and receive the license to manufacture SCALP and Aster-30 missiles, whilst two SAMP/T batteries will be immediately delivered. Ukraine will receive four Rafale aircraft as soon as their pilots and mechanics finish training, which will begin this year, whilst two SAMP/T batteries will be immediately delivered to the nation. The []

description: Ballistic missiles are the one threat Ukraine can't stop with a weapon it makes itself.

🔴 Middle East live: US military launches fresh 'wave of strikes' on Iran
Middle East live: US military launches fresh 'wave of strikes' on Iran
description: The US military on Wednesday launched a fresh "wave of strikes" on Iran, CENTCOM announced. News of the strikes came after an Iranian government spokesperson said more than 30 civilians were killed in US air strikes targeting the country’s southern region in “recent days”. Tehran was quick to retaliate with Jordan, Kuwait and Bahrain all reporting attacks. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

title: Cody Bellinger stars at MLB All-Star game, wins MVP award
description: With his father - former big leaguer Clay Bellinger - in attendance, the New York Yankees player earned MVP honours.

title: ‘Cycle of chaos’: Israel killing Gaza civil officials to derail its future
description: Israels relentless Gaza attacks aim to sabotage the ceasefire and ensure perpetual chaos, analysts warn.

description: Operation would be a dramatic military spectacle but would carry costs analysts doubt Washington is willing to incur.

description: What are the Israeli withdrawal ‘pilot zones’ in southern Lebanon?

title: Iran threatens to block more trade routes as US launches fresh strikes
description: US President Donald Trump vowed to strike Iran's bridges and power plants next week if the country does not return to talks.

title: The Vague Clause That Helps Explain the U.S.-Iran Fight Over the Strait of Hormuz
description: The memorandum of understanding between the United States and Iran has done little to calm tension, in part because they disagree on what it actually means.

title: New York offers Democrats a roadmap for data center fight
description: New York Gov. Kathy Hochul's first-in-the-nation data center moratorium could provide a playbook for other Democrats confronting one of the midterms' most heated issues.Why it matters: Voters across the country are protesting data centers and looking for politicians to take decisive action to address concerns over the industry's rapid expansion.Until now, no governor had imposed a statewide data center moratorium. Hochul's approach — using execut

title: Israeli firm wants to convert commercial ships into floating drone bases
description: Elbit Systems detailed a new maritime concept centered on its Hermes 650 Spark unmanned aircraft, envisioning the conversion of commercial surface ships.

title: European firms plan exo-atmospheric interceptor for 2027 test
description: Bliksem EXO is meant to defeat ballistic threats including Russia’s intermediate-range Oreshnik missile with separating and maneuvering re-entry vehicles.

title: Kenya's 'Soccer Grannies' take to the pitch amid World Cup fever
description: Although no African teams remain in the World Cup, football fever continues to grip the continent, including among a group of grandmothers in rural Kenya. Across Africa, a growing number of older women are taking up the sport, finding a new path to fitness while challenging stereotypes about aging in the process.

title: Firefighters continue to battle 'contained' Fontainebleau fire near Paris
description: Hundreds of French firefighters on Tuesday managed to contain two fires that have burned over 2,000 hectares of the historic Fontainebleau forest near Paris over the last two days. But the local prefect warned that the fires were not extinguished and firefighters will need to remain on the scene for days, if not weeks.

title: Chinese growth in second quarter weakest since Covid-19 era
description: Official data released on Wednesday shows China's GDP grew 4.3% in the second quarter, compared to a year prior. The last time quarterly growth was that weak was in late 2022, when China was feeling the impact of the Covid pandemic. Slow consumer demand, despite a surge in exports, helps explain the slowdown. Also in this edition, Gibraltar's business community breathes a sigh of relief as the physical border between the British overseas territor

title: EU, UK hit Russia with joint sanctions over cyber attacks
description: The European Union and Britain targeted Russia Monday with coordinated sanctions over cyber attacks in Europe, accusing Moscow's FSB intelligence agency over involvement in digital strikes. Russia in response slammed the accusations as "baseless".

title: Ukraine's PM Svyrydenko resigns in Zelenskiy's latest reshuffle
description: Ukraine's parliament ​accepted the resignation on Tuesday of Prime Minister Yulia Svyrydenko, who stepped down after just a ​year in office at the request of President Volodymyr Zelenskiy, in a reshuffle for which he has offered little explanation. The motion to accept the 40-year-old economist's resignation passed in parliament, although ​some ‌lawmakers expressed unease about such an abrupt change.

title: Iran threatens to cut off major vital seaways in response to Trump's renewed blockade
description: After closing the Strait of Hormuz, Tehran on Wednesday threatened to shut additional energy export routes including the Bab el-Mandeb gateway to the Red Sea, a major global shipping route through which Saudi Arabia exports its oil. The warning comes after the US launched a fresh round of strikes on Iran.

title: Lebanon, Israel hold US-brokered talks in Rome
description: Lebanon and Israel resumed talks on Tuesday in the Italian capital, with Beirut hoping for progress towards securing an Israeli withdrawal from south Lebanon under a US-brokered agreement, although expectations for swift progress were low.

title: Iran 'still has the military capability to create chaos in the region'
description: Days of retaliatory strikes across the Middle East by Iran show that American claims to have largely knocked out Tehran’s military capabilities in the initial phase of the war are false, says France 24’s Kethevane Gorjestani, with Iran still clearly possessing the means “to create chaos in the region”.
